channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.nintendolife.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.nintendolife.com

item.title: Rumour: Nintendo Might Be Updating The Switch 2's LCD Panel
item.content:encoded:
Could another revision be on the way?

It's not uncommon for Nintendo to release subtle hardware revisions, and a new rumour doing the rounds online this week suggests the Japanese firm could be updating the LCD panel on the Switch 2.

Now, before anyone gets too excited, we're not talking about Nintendo swapping the current screen out for a fancy OLED display or anything like that, but according to Nintendo Patents Watch, it appears the video game giant might be updating the device with a new LCD panel. This information comes from a Chinese resale site, which allegedly shows a "new model of the Switch 2 LCD panel", with the specs and display all in line with the existing panel.

The current system apparently uses LCD screens assembled by Innolux, and based on the latest discovery, it's believed Nintendo could be swapping these panels out for ones directly assembled by Sharp. So, it may not be a massive overhaul or revision, but it could potentially result in an improved experience for users in handheld mode. To add to this rumour, a recent financial report from Sharp said the company's Hakusan plant had expanded sales for mobile applications.

Of course, nothing has been officially confirmed, so it's best to treat this as a rumour for now. If this is just a panel update, there will likely be no easily noticeable cosmetic difference. Although, the same source notes the "exposed circuit, connector, and cables are significantly different" from the launch model, which could suggest something more. This follows news earlier this month, when Nintendo confirmed a Switch 2 revision was planned for Europe, allowing users to "easily" replace the battery.

When the Switch 2 launched last June, there were mixed reactions to the system's LCD panel and even reports about people having issues with motion blurring and ghosting. We said the screen was "decent" and "enough of a size and refresh-rate upgrade to not feel like a massive downstep from the OLED model" Switch.

If we hear any significant developments, we'll provide an update.

[source bsky.app, via nintendoeverything.com]

Liam is a news writer and reviewer on Nintendo Life. He's been writing about games for more than 15 years and is a lifelong fan of many iconic video game characters.

  • Author Profile
  • Bluesky
  • Reply


https://www.nintendolife.com/news/2026/06/rumour-nintendo-might-be-updating-the-switch-2s-lcd-panel
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: More Mario Kart World 'Free Roam' Songs Have Been Added To Nintendo Music
item.content:encoded:
Featuring tracks from Mario Kart DS & Wii

Nintendo recently added the Mario Kart World soundtrack to its music app, and since then, has been rolling out weekly updates featuring additional tracks from the game's 'Free Roam' mode.

This latest update today Free Roam songs based on the 'Mario Kart Wii' and 'Mario Kart DS' playlist. The DS playlist includes 12 tracks with 33 minutes of runtime, and the Wii playlist has 13 tracks with 37 minutes of runtime.

Here are the latest songs included in this update:

Free Roam with Mario Kart Wii (Mario Kart World)

  1. Luigi Circuit
  2. Mushroom Gorge
  3. Coconut Mall
  4. DK's Snowboard Cross
  5. Wario's Gold Mine
  6. Daisy Circuit (Acoustic)
  7. Daisy Circuit (Electro)
  8. Maple Treeway
  9. Grumble Volcano
  10. Dry Dry Ruins
  11. Moonview Highway
  12. Rainbow Road
  13. Staff Credits A

Free Roam with Mario Kart DS (Mario Kart World)

  1. Figure-8 Circuit
  2. Yoshi Falls
  3. Cheep Cheep Beach
  4. Luigi's Mansion
  5. Delfino Square
  6. Waluigi Pinball
  7. Waluigi Pinball (Funk)
  8. Shroom Ridge
  9. Tick-Tock Clock
  10. Peach Gardens
  11. Rainbow Road
  12. Staff Credits

This latest Mario Kart World soundtrack update follows multiple other 'Free Roam' songs being added to the service. If you want to listen to these tracks on Nintendo Music, you'll need to have an active Switch Online subscription. Nintendo has also recently rolled out a browser version of Nintendo Music.

What do you think of the latest Nintendo Music update? What other albums would you like to see? Let us know in the comments.

Liam is a news writer and reviewer on Nintendo Life. He's been writing about games for more than 15 years and is a lifelong fan of many iconic video game characters.

  • Author Profile
  • Bluesky
  • Reply
